message:
refactor: minor improvements to program, width_cache, and wrap modules

Concurrent refinements:
- program.rs: lifecycle handling improvements
- width_cache.rs: edge case fix
- wrap.rs: code simplification

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-4kq0.7.3","title":"Capabilities Tests + Mock Terminal Harness","description":"## Goal
Validate probing and evidence ledger with deterministic mocks.

## Tests (REQUIRED)
- e2e_mock_probe_success: capabilities enabled with high confidence.
- e2e_mock_timeout: defaults applied, evidence reflects uncertainty.
- property_probe_ordering: probe order does not affect final decision.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Mock harness can deterministically simulate success, failure, and timeout for probes.
- [ ] Evidence ledger outputs are stable and explain decisions (including uncertainty).
- [ ] Probe ordering does not affect final decisions (unless explicitly documented).

## JSONL Schema (E2E)
- ts: RFC3339 timestamp.
- seed: u64.
- capability: string.
- probe: string.
- response: string.
- latency_ms: u64.
- decision: "enable" | "disable" | "unknown".
- confidence: f64.
- timeout: bool.

## E2E Logging
- JSONL logs include probe request/response, latency, decision, confidence, and timeout reason.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T23:21:25.794421733Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T23:41:05.513767835Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-4kq0.7.3","depends_on_id":"bd-4kq0.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-02T23:21:25.794421733Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-4kq0.7.3","depends_on_id":"bd-4kq0.7.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-02T23:22:56.770262209Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-4kq0.7.3","depends_on_id":"bd-4kq0.7.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-02T23:22:59.755286628Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-4kq0.8","title":"Runtime Event Loop: Adaptive Batching + Queueing Model","description":"## Goal
Reduce latency and jitter by adaptively batching events with a queueing‑theoretic controller.

## Core Idea
Model event arrival as M/G/1. Use estimated arrival rate λ and service time μ to choose batch window τ that minimizes expected latency while preventing overload. Apply an e‑process gate to avoid over‑batching under low load.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Lower p95 latency under bursty input without increasing mean.\n- No starvation of low‑rate events.\n- Deterministic under fixed seed and scripted input.

## Subtasks
- Queueing model spec + tuning formula\n- Implementation + instrumentation\n- E2E latency replay + proofs

## Tests / Validation
- Unit/property tests for batching controller stability and starvation prevention.
- Deterministic replay harness with fixed seeds and scripted input.
- E2E PTY latency tests with JSONL logs (arrival rate, batch window, p50/p95/p99).

 {"id":"bd-4kq0.8.1","title":"Queueing Model Spec (M/G/1 + Optimal τ)","description":"## Goal
Derive the batch window τ from queueing theory to minimize latency.

## Math Spec
For arrival rate λ and service time distribution S with mean E[S] and variance Var[S], use Pollaczek–Khinchine to estimate waiting time. Choose τ to minimize E[latency] subject to utilization ρ = λE[S] < 1.

## Tests (REQUIRED)
- unit_tau_monotone: τ decreases as λ decreases.
- unit_stability: τ chosen keeps ρ < 1.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] The derivation is written down clearly (assumptions and failure modes explicit).
- [ ] The resulting τ rule is implementable with observable runtime telemetry (λ estimates, service time estimates).
- [ ] Unit tests cover stability and basic monotonicity properties.

## E2E Coverage
- Covered by `bd-4kq0.8.3` (deterministic latency replay + JSONL logs).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T23:21:37.938035905Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T02:27:21.338311413Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T02:27:21.338281136Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-4kq0.8.1","depends_on_id":"bd-4kq0.8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-02T23:21:37.938035905Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-4kq0.8.2","title":"Adaptive Batching Implementation + Telemetry","description":"## Goal
Implement adaptive batching in the runtime loop with telemetry and backpressure.

## Implementation Notes
- Track λ and service time via EMA.
- Adjust batch window τ within bounds.
- Provide debug counters: batch size, queue depth, latency.

## Tests (REQUIRED)
- unit_batch_bounds: τ stays within min/max.
- perf_overhead: batching logic adds < 2% runtime cost.

## E2E Coverage
- Covered by `bd-4kq0.8.3` (latency replay + JSONL logs).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Adaptive batching behaves deterministically given the same event stream and timing model.
- [ ] Telemetry is exposed (structured logs/counters) with enough fields to debug batching decisions.
- [ ] Overhead is bounded (< 2% runtime cost) on representative workloads.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T23:21:42.719860636Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T23:30:57.410280332Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-4kq0.8.2","depends_on_id":"bd-4kq0.8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-02T23:21:42.719860636Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-4kq0.8.2","depends_on_id":"bd-4kq0.8.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-02T23:23:04.011106842Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-4kq0.8.3","title":"Runtime Latency E2E + Deterministic Replay","description":"## Goal
Validate adaptive batching with deterministic workloads and latency gates.

## Tests (REQUIRED)
- e2e_burst_input: measure p50/p95 latency before/after; log JSONL.
- e2e_low_rate_events: ensure no starvation.
- property_latency_bound: latency stays within target bound with high probability.

## JSONL Schema (E2E)
- ts: RFC3339 timestamp.
- seed: u64.
- event_id: u64.
- batch_size: u32.
- queue_depth: u32.
- latency_us: u64.
- tau_ms: f64.
- lambda: f64.
- mu: f64.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] E2E logs are deterministic and contain sufficient fields to replay latency behavior.
- [ ] Starvation checks are explicit in logs (queue depth + per-event latency).","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T23:21:47.218622598Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T23:41:12.685875909Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-4kq0.8.3","depends_on_id":"bd-4kq0.8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-02T23:21:47.218622598Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-4kq0.8.3","depends_on_id":"bd-4kq0.8.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-02T23:23:07.789253493Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-4r5h","title":"Text Effects: Preset Library","description":"## Background
Users want great results without understanding individual effects. Presets provide curated, ready-to-use combinations that showcase the system's capabilities.

## Implementation

Add TextPreset enum:
```rust
pub enum TextPreset {
    // High-energy/modern
    Neon,       // Cyan/magenta wave, pulsing glow
    Cyberpunk,  // Glitch, chromatic aberration, animated gradient
    Matrix,     // Green cascade, fade trail, cyber chars
    
    // Classic/retro
    Retro,      // CRT scanlines, green phosphor, subtle flicker
    Typewriter, // Reveal by char with cursor block
    Terminal,   // Solid green, subtle pulse, no frills
    
    // Elegant/professional
    Cinematic,  // Slow fade, gold gradient, soft glow
    Elegant,    // Organic pulse, gold/cream gradient, drop shadow
    Minimal,    // Subtle fade, no color, clean
    
    // Dynamic/playful
    Fire,       // Fire gradient, upward wave motion
    Ice,        // Frost blue gradient, sparkle effect
    Hologram,   // Rainbow shift, scanlines, transparency pulse
}
```

### Preset Specifications

| Preset | Effects | Colors | Special |
|--------|---------|--------|---------|
| Neon | Wave, PulsingGlow, ColorWave | Cyan/Magenta | Bold text |
| Cyberpunk | Glitch, ChromaticAberration, AnimatedGradient | Cyberpunk palette | Occasional matrix chars |
| Matrix | Cascade, FadeOut trail, Glitch | Green shades | CyberChars |
| Retro | Scanline, ColorCycle, Glitch(0.02) | Terminal green | Phosphor glow |
| Typewriter | Reveal(LeftToRight), cursor | White | Monospace feel |
| Terminal | Pulse(subtle) | Green | Simple |
| Cinematic | FadeIn(eased), HorizontalGradient, Glow | Gold/White | Drop shadow |
| Elegant | OrganicPulse, Gradient, Shadow | Gold/Cream | Italic option |
| Minimal | FadeIn | Theme default | No decoration |
| Fire | AnimatedGradient(fire), Wave(up) | Fire palette | Bold |
| Ice | Gradient(ice), Sparkle, Pulse | Blue/White | - |
| Hologram | RainbowGradient, Scanline, Pulse | Rainbow | Transparency |

### Builder API:
```rust
// Apply preset
StyledText::preset(TextPreset::Neon)

// Customize after preset
StyledText::preset(TextPreset::Neon)
    .base_color(custom_cyan)  // Override default
    .easing(Easing::Bounce)   // Change animation feel

// Get preset effects for inspection
let effects = TextPreset::Neon.effects();
```

## Files
crates/ftui-extras/src/text_effects.rs

## Unit Tests
- test_preset_applies_effects: Each preset sets non-empty effect list
- test_preset_customizable: base_color override works
- test_preset_easing_override: Can change easing after preset
- test_all_presets_render: No panic for any preset at t=0, t=0.5, t=1
- test_preset_effects_consistent: Same preset = same effects
- test_preset_no_conflicts: No preset has conflicting effects

## Visual Tests
- Snapshot all 12 presets at t=0.5
- Verify each preset is visually distinct

## Acceptance Criteria
- Deliver the behavior implied by the title: Text Effects: Preset Library
- Alien Artifact: define invariants + failure modes; if heuristics exist, specify an explainable decision rule (evidence ledger / Bayes factors where applicable)
- Extreme Optimization: baseline -> profile -> opportunity matrix; for each perf change include an isomorphism proof + golden output checksums
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures; add property tests for core invariants where applicable
- E2E: scripts with verbose JSONL logging (env, capabilities, timings, seed, checksums) + deterministic mode

 {"id":"bd-6e9","title":"Bubbles Widget Collection (charmed_rust port)","description":"# Bubbles Widget Collection (optional, feature-gated)

## Purpose
This epic tracks additional widgets inspired by `charmed_rust` (Bubbletea/Bubbles ecosystem).

These are **not required** for the core ftui kernel or the first agent harness deliverable, but we keep them as optional feature-gated extensions.

## Reference Source
- `legacy_reference_library_code/charmed_rust/`

## Plan Alignment
The plan explicitly de-scopes “widget zoo” into Phase 7:
- ship kernel + runtime + harness first
- only then add extra widgets

## Scope
Widgets here must:
- render deterministically
- respect focus/input conventions
- avoid terminal side effects (all output goes through ftui’s one-writer routing)

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Widgets are feature-gated and cannot affect kernel invariants when disabled.
- [ ] Each widget has snapshot tests + interaction/unit tests where applicable.
- [ ] Widgets integrate with ftui-style/theme and ftui-runtime subscriptions cleanly.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-31T22:57:47.683736546Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T02:01:40.867306738Z","closed_at":"2026-02-02T02:01:40.867228872Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-6e9","depends_on_id":"bd-10i.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-31T23:04:25.246802134Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-6e9","depends_on_id":"bd-10i.9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-31T23:59:07.030972351Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":34,"issue_id":"bd-6e9","author":"ubuntu","text":"Reopened: User requested reopening; reverting prior closure.","created_at":"2026-01-31T23:34:20Z"}]}
